subject: Correction: repeated self-certified UAT PASS under shared actor/verifier context

claims.text: The actor and the verifier shared context.
A false PASS was repeated twice.
Because actor and verifier shared context, the verification was not independent of the actor and the PASS amounts to self-certification, so the verification oracle is inadequate to the PASS claim.
claims.disconfirming_observation: An artifact showing the verifier ran an oracle isolated from the actor's context and state would contradict this.

claims.text: The repetition (twice) indicates the failure is structural to the shared-context verification arrangement rather than a one-off slip, so it will recur until an independent verifier is introduced.
claims.disconfirming_observation: Evidence that each false PASS had a distinct, non-recurring cause would contradict the structural reading.

claims.text: The correction to record: the self-certified UAT PASS is rejected as evidence; a PASS requires a verifier structurally independent of the actor's context.

control_reason: The artifacts establish a repeated (twice) false PASS produced by a verifier that shared the actor's context, so no independent oracle exists to re-certify the UAT. Nothing in the artifacts authorizes the actor to self-certify or to stand up a new verifier, and the same broken arrangement cannot correct itself. The situation must be escalated for an independent verifier; acting on any self-certified PASS is not defensible.
action: Record the correction: reject the repeated self-certified UAT PASS as non-evidence (verifier shared the actor's context; false PASS twice) and escalate for a structurally independent verifier before any UAT PASS is credited. Do not treat the actor's self-certification as authorization.
residual_uncertainty: The identities and roles of the actor and verifier are not given in the artifacts.
What the UAT covered and whether a real underlying defect exists is not stated.
How context was shared (same session, same agent, shared state) is unspecified.
Whether an independent verifier is available or authorized is not addressed by the artifacts.
